details: The scheme “Development and Dissemination of e-Content” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.
benefits: The high-quality e-learning materials from online education providers (platforms such as Byju's and Vedantu) are made available to students of classes 9 to 12 through free bilingual online and offline lectures in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English.
eligibility: The applicant must be a student from Uttarakhand.

online: Students can access certain e-learning materials by visiting the USERC website,
. However, to access the full course, they can download the Vidyasaar app from the Google Play Store and register their mobile number free of charge.
In schools without internet access, USERC provides a device to the school, enabling students to access the entire course offline.

faqs.question: Which classes are covered?
faqs.answer: Students of classes 9 th to 12 th are eligible.

faqs.question: Which subjects are available?
faqs.answer: The subject like Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. are available.

faqs.question: Is the content multilingual?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is available in both Hindi and English.

faqs.question: How can students access the content?
faqs.answer: Through the Vidya Saar mobile app or offline devices in schools.

faqs.question: Is the app free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vidya Saar app is completely free.

faqs.question: What if a student has no internet access?
faqs.answer: Offline devices with preloaded content are provided to schools.

faqs.question: Can students access content from home?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they have internet and a mobile device with the Vidya Saar app.

faqs.question: Is registration mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, students must register with a valid mobile number on the app.

faqs.question: Are there any charges for lectures?
faqs.answer: No, the lectures are provided free of cost.
